title: Feature: Swarm Agent Personalities — specialized roles with goal-driven objective loop
description: Build 6 specialized agent roles (coder, reviewer, tester, documenter, UX, fixer) with context-aware prompts, goal-driven objective loop that re-evaluates ALL objectives after every action, supervisor orchestration with parallel agent execution, and per-role performance tracking.

phases.name: Agent Prompts
phases.description: System prompt templates for each agent role with role-specific context placeholders

phases.name: Objective System
phases.description: Objective enum, evaluators, and dependency graph for parallel execution

phases.name: Context Assembly
phases.description: AgentContext struct and supervisor context builders per role

phases.name: Agent Implementations
phases.description: Each specialized agent as a pipeline component with structured output

phases.name: Supervisor + Loop
phases.description: Goal-driven loop that assigns agents based on unmet objectives

phases.name: Integration
phases.description: Wire into hex dev, HexFlo task tracking, performance metrics

steps.description: Create 6 agent prompt templates in hex-cli/assets/prompts/: agent-coder.md (step+ports+rules+existing code context), agent-reviewer.md (source+port+review checklist with severity levels), agent-tester.md (source+port+London school patterns+Deps pattern), agent-documenter.md (ADR+all files+workplan+port surface), agent-ux.md (adapter code+UX checklist for CLI/API/frontend), agent-fixer.md (issue+source+port+fix template with upstream agent output). Each template has role-specific {{placeholders}}.

Define Objective enum (CodeGenerated, CodeCompiles, TestsPass, ReviewPasses, ArchitectureGradeA, UxReviewPasses, DocsGenerated) and ObjectiveState struct (met, detail, blocking_issues). Define objective_dependencies() function returning which objectives must be met before others can be evaluated (e.g., CodeCompiles before TestsPass).

Implement objective evaluators. Each Objective gets an evaluate() method that checks current state: CodeCompiles runs tsc/cargo check, TestsPass runs bun test/cargo test, ReviewPasses checks for review result file, ArchitectureGradeA calls GET /api/analyze, DocsGenerated checks README.md exists. Returns ObjectiveState with blocking_issues for the fixer. evaluate_all() runs all evaluators and returns the full state vector.

Create AgentContext struct (prompt_template, source_files, port_interfaces, boundary_rules, workplan_step, upstream_output, metadata) and context builder methods on Supervisor: build_coder_context (step+ports+existing code), build_reviewer_context (source+port+checklist), build_tester_context (source+port+test patterns), build_documenter_context (ADR+all files+workplan), build_ux_context (adapter code+UX checklist), build_fixer_context (issue+source+fix template). Each builder reads actual files from output_dir.

Implement hex-reviewer agent. Takes AgentContext, calls inference with agent-reviewer.md template, parses structured ReviewResult: verdict (PASS/NEEDS_FIXES), issues[] with severity (critical/warning/info), file, line, description, fix_suggestion. Uses deepseek-r1 for reasoning. Writes review result to output_dir/.hex-review/<step-id>.json.

Implement hex-tester agent. Takes AgentContext, calls inference with agent-tester.md template, generates test file following London school (no mock.module, Deps pattern injection). Writes test to output_dir/tests/. Then runs compile_check + run_tests. Returns TestAgentResult: test_file_path, pass/fail counts, failure output for fixer context.

Implement hex-documenter agent. Takes AgentContext with ADR + all source files + workplan + port interfaces. Generates: README.md (overview from ADR, hex architecture diagram, quick start with install/run/test commands, API reference from port interfaces, development guide). Also generates inline JSDoc/rustdoc by reading each public file and adding doc comments. Uses llama-4-maverick (fast structured output).

Implement hex-ux agent. Takes AgentContext with adapter code + user description. Reviews: CLI (help text, consistent flags, error messages with suggestions), API (consistent response shapes, proper HTTP status codes, pagination, error format), Frontend (keyboard navigation, color contrast, responsive). Returns UxReviewResult with issues + recommendations. Only activated when workplan has tier 2 primary adapters. Uses deepseek-r1 for UX reasoning.

Implement the goal-driven objective loop in Supervisor::run_tier(). For each tier: (1) evaluate_all objectives, (2) find first unmet objective, (3) determine which agent can fix it (CodeCompiles→fixer, TestsPass→tester or fixer, ReviewPasses→reviewer or fixer, etc.), (4) build context for that agent, (5) execute agent, (6) re-evaluate ALL objectives (not just the one fixed), (7) repeat until all met or max_iterations reached. Max 5 iterations per tier.

Add parallel agent execution within the objective loop. When multiple unmet objectives are independent (no dependency edge between them), run their agents in parallel. Example: ReviewPasses + UxReviewPasses can run concurrently. Use the objective dependency graph from step-2 to determine parallelism. Execute parallel agents via tokio::join!.

Wire Supervisor into hex dev headless + TUI modes. Replace direct code_phase + validate_phase in run_headless() with Supervisor::run(workplan, output_dir, config). In --auto: all agents run without gates. In interactive: gate after review (show issues), gate after test failures. In --quick: skip reviewer + documenter + UX, only coder + compile + analyze.

Create HexFlo tasks for each agent assignment. Each agent execution creates a swarm task with agent_role, context_hash, upstream_tasks. Task status tracks: assigned → running → completed/failed. Dashboard shows per-agent task stream with role colors. hex report shows agent assignments per tier.

Track per-role performance in SpacetimeDB. Extend model_performance with agent_role field. Record success/failure/iterations per role per model. Dashboard: per-role success rate chart. RL learns which models are best for reviewing vs coding vs testing. hex report --summary shows aggregate role performance.
